content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Accepted 1 amendment: the current `task select` change implicitly persists `active-milestone`, and because omitted milestone commands consult that pointer before deriving from the active project, it can silently retarget later milestone operations after subsequent selects.

## Votes

- `fr-3-ef7edfaf`: Accept
  `src/cli/task.rs` now routes `task select` through `project::select_active_project`, which in turn calls `sync_active_milestone_from_project_record`. In `src/contexts/workspace_governance/mod.rs`, that helper writes `active-milestone` only when the selected project has a milestone and does not clear the pointer when a later selection has none. `src/cli/milestone.rs` resolves omitted milestone IDs from `read_active_milestone()` before falling back to `active_project_milestone_id()`, so once `task select` seeds a valid milestone pointer, later `milestone ...` commands can keep targeting that older milestone even after the user selects a plain project. The added test in `tests/cli.rs` explicitly locks in this new side effect on the preferred `task` surface. Even if `project select` already has the same semantics, extending that hidden state mutation to `task select` is a real correctness regression unless the milestone-clearing behavior is made explicit.
